title: pseudophegopteris pallida
intro: Pseudophegopteris pallida, also known as the pale false spleenwort, is a type of fern that belongs to the genus Pseudophegopteris. It is a non-vascular plant species that is native to New Zealand. Pseudophegopteris pallida has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas or infusions to utilize its health benefits, which include relieving coughs and sore throats.
